name: preconditions_vacuous
code:
proof fn phi_1_preconditions_vacuous(os: OS, sender_thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, blocking_endpoint_index: EndpointIdx)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.thread_dom().contains(sender_thread_ptr),
        0 <= blocking_endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
        os.get_thread(sender_thread_ptr).state == ThreadState::RUNNING,
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: If provable, the preconditions are jointly unsatisfiable, making the function vacuously correct but uncallable

name: always_returns_error
code:
proof fn phi_2_always_returns_error(os_pre: OS, os_post: OS, sender_thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, blocking_endpoint_index: EndpointIdx, ret: SyscallReturnStruct)
    requires
        os_pre.wf(),
        os_pre.thread_dom().contains(sender_thread_ptr),
        0 <= blocking_endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
        os_pre.get_thread(sender_thread_ptr).state == ThreadState::RUNNING,
        os_post.wf(),
    ensures
        ret == SyscallReturnStruct::NoSwitchNew(RetValueType::Error),
{
}
reason: If provable, the ensures clause (only self.wf()) is so weak it cannot rule out the function always reporting failure and never delivering a message

name: sender_thread_lost
code:
proof fn phi_3_sender_thread_lost(os_pre: OS, os_post: OS, sender_thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, blocking_endpoint_index: EndpointIdx)
    requires
        os_pre.wf(),
        os_pre.thread_dom().contains(sender_thread_ptr),
        0 <= blocking_endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
        os_pre.get_thread(sender_thread_ptr).state == ThreadState::RUNNING,
        os_post.wf(),
    ensures
        !os_post.thread_dom().contains(sender_thread_ptr),
{
}
reason: If provable, the spec permits the sender thread to be silently removed from the thread domain by a non-blocking send

name: receiver_never_exists
code:
proof fn phi_4_receiver_never_exists(os: OS, sender_thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, blocking_endpoint_index: EndpointIdx)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.thread_dom().contains(sender_thread_ptr),
        0 <= blocking_endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
        os.get_thread(sender_thread_ptr).state == ThreadState::RUNNING,
    ensures
        !os.receiver_exist(sender_thread_ptr, blocking_endpoint_index),
{
}
reason: If provable, the well-formedness invariant structurally precludes any waiting receiver, forcing every send to take an error path

name: endpoint_index_degenerate
code:
proof fn phi_5_endpoint_index_degenerate(os: OS, sender_thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, blocking_endpoint_index: EndpointIdx)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.thread_dom().contains(sender_thread_ptr),
        0 <= blocking_endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
        os.get_thread(sender_thread_ptr).state == ThreadState::RUNNING,
    ensures
        blocking_endpoint_index == 0,
{
}
reason: If provable, MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS is at most 1, meaning each thread can reference only a single endpoint — a severe capability limitation
